events.title: The Landlord's Grips
events.scene: The landlord's agent, a grim man named Vasily, stands at your door, his breath misting in the cold air. The meager harvest has made rent impossible, and the scent of lamp-oil from your small home seems too precious to waste. Your family, the {surname}s, huddle, listening.
events.researchNote: Jewish families in the Pale often rented land and homes from gentiles, facing precarious tenancy.

choices.text: Bargain with Vasily, promising what little you have and hoping for leniency, trusting the community's support.
choices.outcome: Vasily takes what he can, grumbling. The community offers what little they have, tightening your bonds but leaving you poorer.

choices.text: Use the saved passage money, sent by a distant cousin, to send a younger {family_name} member to America.
choices.outcome: A tearful farewell. The hope of a better life, but the pain of a severed family bond, hangs heavy in the air.

events.title: The Rabbi's Plea
events.scene: The air in the Beit Midrash is thick with the smell of old books and lamp-oil, the murmur of study a constant hum. Rabbi Mendel, his face etched with worry, speaks of growing dangers and the need for the community, including the {surname}s, to stand together.
events.researchNote: The synagogue and religious leaders were central to Jewish life and survival in the Pale, especially amidst rising antisemitism.

choices.text: Pledge your family's full support to Rabbi Mendel's call for traditional communal solidarity and charity.
choices.outcome: Your family earns deep respect within the shtetl, becoming pillars of the established order, but perhaps too resistant to change.

choices.text: Argue for a more proactive, even political, response to the threats, hinting at new movements beyond the shtetl.
choices.outcome: Some are inspired by your resolve, others are wary of unsettling the delicate peace. The {family_name} name becomes associated with outspokenness.

events.title: The Monopolka's Shadow
events.scene: The Imperial government's vodka monopoly, the 'Monopolka', makes legal spirits expensive and scarce. A hushed conversation after dark, by the faint glow of a smuggled lantern, offers the {full_name} family a chance to earn by illicitly distilling and distributing alcohol.
events.researchNote: The state alcohol monopoly (monopolka) in the Russian Empire was a major source of revenue and a target for illegal production and trade.

choices.text: Take the risk. The profits are high, but so is the danger of discovery by the Tsar's agents.
choices.outcome: A steady, if dangerous, flow of silver begins. The {family_name} name gains a shadowy influence, whispered in certain corners.

choices.text: Refuse the offer. Poverty is harsh, but a clear conscience and good name are invaluable.
choices.outcome: Your family remains above reproach, respected by the honest folk, but the gnawing hunger for more lingers.

events.title: Beyond the Shtetl Walls
events.scene: The world beyond the Pale is changing rapidly. Whispers of new sciences, burgeoning industries, and secular universities reach even the remote shtetl. The cold off the road hints at a wider world, and {given_name}, or another bright {family_name} child, shows an uncommon curiosity.
events.researchNote: The late 19th century saw increasing secularization and desire for modern education among some Jewish youth in the Pale.

choices.text: Focus on traditional Torah study, ensuring the child becomes a scholar and upholder of religious law.
choices.outcome: The child delves deep into ancient texts, bringing honor to the {family_name} name within the religious community.

choices.text: Seek out secular education or a modern apprenticeship, perhaps in a larger town, for a child with a keen mind.
choices.outcome: A new path opens, leading to potential influence and wealth in the wider world, but with some raised eyebrows in the shtetl.
